_meta.name: CLM r5-a10 train_clm.hexa per-step leak fix — 2026-04-20
_meta.purpose: Document the three identified per-step leaks in train_step, the fixes applied, smoke verification result, and residual blocker for r5-a10.5

state_reason: Three structural per-step leaks identified and fixed in train_clm.hexa. Build OK, smoke partially OK (step 0 reaches in 75s vs prior never; leak rate cut 3.4GB/min → 1.5GB/min). However 1.5GB/min still > 200MB/min PASS gate, so full 20K-step launch BLOCKED per task spec. Residual leak source identified as hexa runtime list op churn in decoder_forward 12-layer stack — requires r5-a10.5 deeper refactor.

diagnostic_run.matches_prior_oom: yes — original pid 1690453 OOMed at 117GB after 52min @ batch=2 seq=512; this 38min projection at batch=1 seq=256 is consistent (smaller batch = less compute-time per step but same per-step alloc churn = similar RSS curve)

before.behavior: Calls corpus_open(G_CORPUS_PATH) every invocation. corpus_open does exec("wc -c < ...") + println("[clm_mmap_loader] opened ..."). Called from train_step's H1 inline window reader (line 3084) AND from _make_batch fallback (line 3050). At ~10s/step that's ~6 forks/min + ~6 println string concats/min.

after.behavior: If G_CORPUS_INIT==1 (load_corpus has run, scalars populated), pack CorpusDesc directly from G_CORPUS_PATH/G_CORPUS_LEN — zero IO, zero subprocess. Falls through to legacy corpus_open() only when G_CORPUS_INIT==0 (selftest path).
after.verified_in_smoke: yes — only ONE [clm_mmap_loader] opened ... line in smoke log (the init from load_corpus), zero per-step occurrences

three_leaks_identified.title: C41 GWT + Holo head per-step matrix allocation

before.behavior: _c41_gwt_head_new(C41_N_DOMAINS=12, d_model=768, 137 + state.step) + _c41_holo_head_new(d_model=768, d_model*2=1536, 241 + state.step) called every step with seed=137+step. Each calls _c41_det_rand which does N list.push() of float values. Per step: 12*768 + 768*768 = 9216 + 589824 = 599040 push() calls.

after.behavior: Three module-scope cache vars (G_C41_GWT_PROJ, G_C41_GWT_GATE, G_C41_HOLO_SCRAM) populated ONCE on first train_step call (deterministic seed 137 / 241 — was speculative per-step variation). Per-step we just construct cheap struct skin {n_domains, d_model, project, gate} pointing to cached arrays. Re-init only if d_model differs (selftest path uses d=16, training uses d=768).

before.behavior: For each of seq=256 iterations: (1) build row via 768 pushes, (2) call _c41_holo_encode which does 1536 pushes building bulk, (3) push 768 elements onto boundary_stream + 768 onto interior_stream. Total: 256 × (768+1536+768+768) = ~983k push() calls per step at d_model=768. Hexa runtime push() reallocates underlying buffer each call → cumulative O(N²) work and intermediate buffers leaked.

after.behavior: Pre-allocate boundary_stream + interior_stream as _tz(seq*db) ONCE. Inline holo encode skipping the unused `db` portion of bulk (only `ds` interior values are actually consumed via `bulk[db + (i%ds)]`). For each row: compute interior_vals via _tz(ds) + index assignment. Stream writes via index assignment instead of push. Per-step push count drops from ~983k to ~0 (only _tz init pushes survive, those are single contiguous buffers).
after.verified_in_smoke: yes — step 0 phi_holo=558.583 (matches baseline; semantics preserved)

smoke_run.config: batch=2 seq=512 / steps=50 / save_every=50 / corpus=/workspace/corpus_clm_r4.txt (5.24MB symlink to /root/Dev/anima/data/corpus.txt)
smoke_run.pass_gate_status: FAIL — 1.5 GB/min still 7.5x over 200 MB/min threshold
smoke_run.kill_reason: manual SIGTERM after confirming residual leak rate steady at 1.5GB/min; would have OOMed at 117GB after ~78min if left running

full_launch_decision.reason: smoke FAIL per task spec rule: 'smoke FAIL or RSS curve still 1GB/min+ leak → no full launch, additional diagnosis'

residual_blocker.title: Hexa runtime list-op leak in decoder_forward 12-block stack
evidence: Even after eliminating all three identified train_step-level leaks (corpus desc cache + c41 head cache + phi_holo refactor), RSS still grows 1.5GB/min steady-state.
Process state RNl (running, low priority, multi-thread) — CPU-bound, not IO/syscall-blocked.
Step 1 wallclock unbounded (>3min on hetzner CPU BLAS) — the residual leak compound with BLAS-bound matmul time.
Decoder forward path: 12 layers × _tz + _tslice + _matmul + _tadd + _block_attn + _block_ffn = ~150-300 fresh list allocations per step, each via _tz which uses .push() internally (line 2180-2185).

candidate_fixes.title: Replace _tz with native runtime zero-fill primitive
candidate_fixes.rationale: _tz(n) currently does N .push(0.0) — same O(N²) reallocation pattern. A native [float; n] primitive would be O(N) single alloc.
candidate_fixes.scope: 1 function modification + hexa runtime support check (may already exist as `_alloc_float_buffer` or similar)

candidate_fixes.title: Profile hexa runtime list operations under steady-state
candidate_fixes.rationale: Need empirical data on which op leaks most — push, _tslice, _tadd, _matmul (which uses alloc_raw/free_raw and should be clean). Currently inferring from code structure.
candidate_fixes.scope: Add per-op alloc counters to hexa runtime debug build, run smoke for 60s, dump counts.
candidate_fixes.expected_impact: Identifies the true dominant leak source for surgical fix

candidate_fixes.title: Move full forward + aux to FFI (single C-side step kernel)
candidate_fixes.rationale: _matmul already uses alloc_raw/free_raw correctly. If we marshal entire decoder forward + c41 aux + phi_holo into one C call, the leak surface is gone.
candidate_fixes.scope: Major — requires writing libhx_clm_step.so with C-side decoder + aux ops. Likely a week-scale effort.
candidate_fixes.expected_impact: Eliminates residual leak entirely; also unblocks GPU path via libhxcuda.so dispatch per r5-a6.5

candidate_fixes.title: Wait for r5-a6.5 libhxcuda + run on H100 (skip CPU CLM r5 entirely)
candidate_fixes.rationale: Per parent JSON launch_decision_audit, 1B+ training requires GPU anyway. Residual CPU leak only matters for 170M smoke; if we accept hetzner CPU CLM as 'unviable substrate' and route 170M to ubu RTX 5070 + libhxcuda, the leak is not on the critical path.
candidate_fixes.scope: Depends on r5-a6.5 (libhxcuda.so dispatch) which is already in the queue.
candidate_fixes.expected_impact: Sidesteps the CPU leak by switching substrate; also gives 50-100x speedup.

residual_blocker.recommended: r5-a10.5-D as primary path (route to GPU once r5-a6.5 lands), r5-a10.5-A as parallel hardening (cheap to attempt, useful for any future CPU-only CLM run)

commits_planned.subject: fix(clm): r5-a10 train_clm per-step leak hardening — corpus desc cache + c41 head reuse + phi_holo _tz/index-assignment refactor
